image_dependency: Question explicitly says 'Use the graph of f shown on [0,7]'
The needed information is the behavior/sign of f from a graph, which is not recoverable from question text alone
multi_step: Solving requires connecting G(x)=∫_2^{3x-1} f(t)dt to derivative behavior via the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus and the chain rule
A local maximum of G requires identifying where f(3x-1) changes sign appropriately, then mapping the graph location back to x
This combines calculus reasoning with visual reading of the graph rather than a single direct lookup
Multiple-choice format lowers open-ended complexity somewhat
verifiability: Answer is a specific numeric expression ('4/3'), which is easy to compare against a gold answer
choices are provided and the answer_text matches one choice exactly
The reasoning is rule-checkable in principle: differentiate G, inspect graph sign change, solve for x
registry_passed=true, though asset issues indicate some source ambiguity so score is not maximal

agent_assessment.rationale: The problem text is complete and the graph is simple enough to read despite the small image size: f is positive before x=3, crosses 0 at x=3, then becomes negative. Since G'(x)=3f(3x-1), the local maximum occurs when 3x-1=3, giving x=4/3, which matches the provided answer choice.
